creator: sigehiro@ee.oit.ac.jp
text:
The following command
    % xwd | xwdtopnm > foo.ppm
generates an illegal PPM file on my machine
with 16 bits/pixel display (XF86_Mach64.xtt).

Fix: 

In `/usr/ports/graphics/netpbm/work/netpbm/pnm/xwdtopnm.c',
16 bits/pixel data format is (maybe) considered
to be `0BBBBBGGGGGRRRRR' (see lines 94--114 of xwdtopnm.c).
However, the format is (maybe) `RRRRRGGGGGGBBBBB' on my computer,
and there may exist other formats on other computers.
Please modify the source code correctly ?
(on the basis of variables `{red,green,blue}_mask'
 in the source code.)
How-To-Repeat: On the X Window System (XF86_Mach64.xtt) with 16 bits/pixel display,
execute following command
    % xwd | xwdtopnm > foo.ppm
and display the image `foo.ppm'.
